override_gap.method: Unpaired bootstrap, N=10000. Within each model, resample questions with replacement separately within has_cultural_override=True vs False groups on Other-5 systems; compute mean EM difference; average across the 3 open-source models for each bootstrap iteration. CI is the 2.5/97.5 percentile of the resulting averaged-gap distribution. NOTE: the 23.6pp point in the paper was computed from a 6-model average (3 open + GPT-4o-mini, Gemini-2.5-Flash, Claude-3.5-Haiku); restricted to the 3 open-source models the gap is larger (~30pp).

fictional_real_gap.method: Unpaired bootstrap, N=10000 (different question instances after system/term replacement, so paired pairing is not appropriate). Within each model, resample real and fictional Cat.4 questions on Other-5 systems with replacement separately; compute mean EM difference (fictional - real); average across the 3 open-source models per iteration. CI is 2.5/97.5 percentile.

hop_matched_2hop.method: Unpaired bootstrap, N=10000. Within each model, separately resample Cat.2 questions (at 2-hop, all 7 systems) and Cat.4 questions (at 2-hop, Other-5 systems) with replacement; compute mean EM difference (Cat.2 - Cat.4); average across the 3 open-source models per iteration. CI is 2.5/97.5 percentile.

hop_matched_3hop.method: Unpaired bootstrap, N=10000. Within each model, separately resample Cat.2 questions (at 3-hop, all 7 systems) and Cat.4 questions (at 3-hop, Other-5 systems) with replacement; compute mean EM difference (Cat.2 - Cat.4); average across the 3 open-source models per iteration. CI is 2.5/97.5 percentile.

cat1_average.method: Per-question Cat.1 exact-match rate computed for each of the 5 models with zero-shot-direct numbers (Qwen3-32B, Gemma3-27B, DeepSeek-R1-32B from results_<model>_zero_shot_direct/; GPT-4o-mini from results_gpt4o/ and Gemini-2.5-Flash from results_gemini/, which contain the only zero-shot-direct closed-source data). Cat.1 average is the unweighted mean of the 5 per-model rates.
